---
title: "Multivariate,Triple_dichotomy_refinedaxes_Only_Albuminaxes"
author: "Azadeh"
format: html
editor: visual
---

#Package

```{r}

# remotes::install_github("erikerhardt/erikmisc")

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(car)
library(broom)
library(emmeans)
library(pROC)
library(rlang)
library(patchwork)
library(erikmisc)
```

#load data

```{r}
 # If needed load dat_all_combine
# load("../../../Data/ADRC_VCI_MarkVCID2_ALL_combine.RData")

load("dat_sub_Data_inflammation_analysis_paper_06052025.RData")
load("dat_sub_and_Var_for_inflammation_analysis.RData")


# 1. outcome variable
y <- c("fl_albumin_index_log2", "mri_perm_3t_tot")

# 2. predictors to scale (everything in CSF / plasma **except** the outcome)
vars_to_scale <- setdiff(c(Var$csf, Var$plasma, Var$mri), y)

dat_sheet <- dat_sub %>% 
  mutate(
    across(all_of(vars_to_scale),
           ~ as.numeric(scale(.x)))
  )        # y stays on its original log2 scale

# 176 subjects , filter on baseline
```

##Do any fluid biomarkers correlate with the VD axis?Do any fluid biomarkers correlate with the AD axis?

Note: We will associate plasma biomarkers or their ratios with CSF as the "ground truth" using the model: CSF AD \~ Plasma x1 + Plasma x2 + Plasma x3

Additionally, we filter data based on the Baseline visit in the RedCap event name. Good news---there is still a substantial amount of data available.

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Define the baseline visit categories
baseline_visits <- c(
  "Baseline (Arm 1: Patients)",
  "Baseline (Arm 2: Control)",
  "Baseline (Arm 3: CSF controls)",
  "Baseline (Arm 4: Diabetes)",
  "baseline"
)

# Filter dataset based on baseline visits
dat_all_combine_AD_VD <- dat_all_combine |>
  filter(redcap_event_name %in% baseline_visits) |>  # Keep only baseline visits
  mutate(
    TripleDichotomy_VD = mri_dti_psmd_z,
    TripleDichotomy_VD_log2 = e_log_shift(TripleDichotomy_VD),
    TripleDichotomy_AD = (fl_csf__ALL__ptau / fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42),
    TripleDichotomy_AD_log2 = log2(TripleDichotomy_AD),
    fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2 = log2(fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42/fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40)
  )
# nrow(dat_all_combine_AD_VD)
# Check unique values after filtering on Baseline 
# unique(dat_all_combine_AD_VD$redcap_event_name)
 # hist(dat_all_combine_AD_VD$TripleDichotomy_VD_log2)
### YES YES YES Now 630 rows!!!! YAY!!!!!!!
 # hist(dat_all_combine_AD_VD$TripleDichotomy_AD, 100)



```

# Find all plasma biomarker combinations with albumin

 Note: we check the  Multivariate
1. CSF: Remove IGG.  "IGG is confusing, the other biomarkers are more interesting."
2. CSF-only, add MMP-3 (even if n < 35)

Still the R-square was low 


Running albumin \~ plasma_only

```{r}

library(randomForest)
library(caret)

# Get only plasma biomarkers ending in _log2
find_optimal_log2_groups <- function(data, min_cases = 30) {
  # Get only plasma markers ending in _log2
  plasma_markers <- grep("^fl_plasma.*_log2$", names(data), value = TRUE)
  
  # Start with outcome variable
  current_vars <- c("fl_albumin_index_log2")
  results <- list()
  
  for(marker in plasma_markers) {
    temp_vars <- c(current_vars, marker)
    complete_cases <- sum(complete.cases(data[, temp_vars]))
    
    if(complete_cases >= min_cases) {
      current_vars <- temp_vars
      results[[length(results) + 1]] <- list(
        variables = current_vars,
        complete_cases = complete_cases
      )
    }
  }
  return(results)
}

# Apply the function
log2_groups <- find_optimal_log2_groups(dat_sub)

# Print summary of available biomarkers and sample sizes
for(i in seq_along(log2_groups)) {
  cat("\nGroup", i, ":\n")
  cat("Variables:", paste(log2_groups[[i]]$variables[-1], collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("Complete cases:", log2_groups[[i]]$complete_cases, "\n")
}

# Get the final set of all log2 biomarkers
final_log2_vars <- log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables

# Print final number of biomarkers and sample size
cat("\nFinal model will include:", length(final_log2_vars)-1, "biomarkers\n")
cat("With sample size:", log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$complete_cases, "\n")

# Function to print breakpoint summary
summarize_breakpoints <- function(groups) {
  prev_vars <- NULL
  prev_n <- NULL
  
  cat("Sample Size Breakpoints:\n")
  
  for(i in seq_along(groups)) {
    current_vars <- groups[[i]]$variables[-1]  # exclude outcome variable
    current_n <- groups[[i]]$complete_cases
    
    if(is.null(prev_n) || current_n != prev_n) {
      new_vars <- if(is.null(prev_vars)) {
        current_vars
      } else {
        setdiff(current_vars, prev_vars)
      }
      
      # Clean up variable names for display
      clean_vars <- gsub("fl_plasma__ALL__", "", new_vars)
      clean_vars <- gsub("_log2", "", clean_vars)
      
      cat(sprintf("%d cases: %s\n", 
                  current_n,
                  if(i == 1) {
                    sprintf("starting with %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
                  } else {
                    sprintf("adding %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
                  }))
      
      prev_vars <- current_vars
      prev_n <- current_n
    }
  }
}

# Apply the function to your groups
summarize_breakpoints(log2_groups)

# Also show the complete list of final variables
cat("\nFinal set of biomarkers:\n")
final_vars <- gsub("fl_plasma__ALL__", "", 
                  gsub("_log2", "", 
                       log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables[-1]))
cat(paste(final_vars, collapse = "\n"))



# Apply RandomForest to check the important varibels for Albumin index with 13 Var and 42 Obervation

 biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 # "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",         
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",        
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 # "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,     
 "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)


n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_sheet[, c("fl_albumin_index_log2", biomarkers)]))
print(n_complete) # Only one remained




# Function to run Random Forest analysis
run_rf_analysis <- function(data, outcome_var, predictors, n_trees = 500) {
  # Prepare data
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, predictors)])
  
  # Create formula
  rf_formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Run Random Forest
  set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
  rf_model <- randomForest(
    formula = rf_formula,
    data = model_data,
    ntree = n_trees,
    importance = TRUE
  )

    # Get variable importance
  var_imp <- importance(rf_model)
  var_imp_df <- data.frame(
    variable = gsub("fl_plasma__ALL__", "", gsub("_log2", "", rownames(var_imp))),
    importance = var_imp[, "%IncMSE"],  # Using %IncMSE as importance measure
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  var_imp_df <- var_imp_df[order(-var_imp_df$importance), ]
  
  # Calculate model performance
  predictions <- predict(rf_model) #If no new data is provided, it predicts for the training data.
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2))
  r2 <- 1 - sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2) / 
            sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - mean(model_data[[outcome_var]]))^2)
  
  return(list(
    model = rf_model,
    importance = var_imp_df,
    performance = list(
      rmse = rmse,
      r2 = r2,
      n_obs = nrow(model_data)
    )
  ))
}

# Run Random Forest analysis
rf_results <- run_rf_analysis(
  data = dat_sub,
  outcome_var = "fl_albumin_index_log2",
  predictors = biomarkers
)

# Print results
cat("\nRandom Forest Model Performance:\n")
cat("R-squared:", round(rf_results$performance$r2, 3), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(rf_results$performance$rmse, 3), "\n")
cat("Number of observations:", rf_results$performance$n_obs, "\n")

# Print variable importance
cat("\nVariable Importance (top 10):\n")
print(head(rf_results$importance, 10))

# Plot variable importance
ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# Function to create models with top N variables
create_top_n_model <- function(data, outcome_var, importance_df, n_vars) {
  top_vars <- paste0("fl_plasma__ALL__", 
                    importance_df$variable[1:n_vars], 
                    "_log2")
  
  # Create formula with interactions
  formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", 
                            paste(top_vars, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Fit model
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, top_vars)])
  model <- lm(formula, data = model_data)

    fml_upper <- as.formula(
  paste("outcome_var ~ (", paste(top_vars, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
 
     fml_lower <- as.formula("outcome_var ~ 1")
  
  # 6. Perform stepwise selection

     model_selected <- step(
  object  = model,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower, upper = fml_upper),
  direction = "both",
  trace = 0  # Suppress stepwise output
)

  return(model_selected)
}

# Create models with top 5 variables
top5_model <- create_top_n_model(
  data = dat_sub,
  outcome_var = "fl_albumin_index_log2",
  importance_df = rf_results$importance,
  n_vars = 5
)

# Print results of top 4 model
cat("\nModel with top 5 variables from Random Forest:\n")
summary(top5_model)


# Now base on the result of Random-forest we create new albumin




 
# if you choose this you will get lots of interaction but Rsquare still low around 40%
# 5 Important varibels base on the result of RF IMportance
biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 # "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",         
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",        
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 # "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,     
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2"
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_sheet[, c("fl_albumin_index_log2", biomarkers)]))
print(n_complete) # Only one remained

# sapply(some_biomarkers_vd, function(x) sum(!is.na(dat_sheet[[x]])))



# 2. Build a formula: TripleDichotomy_VD ~ biomarker1 + biomarker2 + ...
albumin_form <- as.formula(
  paste("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "))
)

# Fit main-effects model
albumin_main <- lm(albumin_form, data = dat_sheet)

summary(albumin_main)


# car::Anova(mod_main, type = 3)
# # 
# e_plot_lm_diagnostics(mod_main, sw_plot_set = "simple")
# 
# # Perform Shapiro-Wilk test on the residuals
# shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(residuals(mod_main))


## -- 2. Define the 'upper' and 'lower' scopes for stepwise selection
#    Upper includes two-way interactions: (x1 + x2 + ...)^2
#    Lower is just an intercept-only model: ~ 1

fml_upper_albumin <- as.formula(
  paste("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ (", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
fml_lower_albumin <- as.formula("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ 1")

## -- 3. Run stepwise selection
model_selected_albumin <- step(
  object  = albumin_main,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower_albumin, upper = fml_upper_albumin),
  direction = "both"
)



## -- 4. Inspect the final model
summary(model_selected_albumin)




# Do I need to add plot contrast? regression assumption? add later? 


#─ tidy helpers --------------------------------------------------------------
library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(gt)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

pal_okabe <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
               "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00",
               "#CC79A7", "#999999")

# 1 Break-point data → nice tibble


# turn your `log2_groups` list into a tidy frame
break_df <- imap_dfr(log2_groups, ~{
  tibble(
    order         = .y,
    n_cases       = .x$complete_cases,
    added_vars    = setdiff(.x$variables[-1],
                            if (.y == 1) character(0) else log2_groups[[.y-1]]$variables[-1])
  )
}) %>%
  unnest_longer(added_vars, keep_empty = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(added_vars = str_remove_all(added_vars,
                                     "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"))

# 2 Figure 1: attrition curve
break_plot <- ggplot(break_df,
                     aes(x = order, y = n_cases)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .8, colour = pal_okabe[2]) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour  = pal_okabe[1]) +
  geom_text(aes(label = added_vars),
            hjust = -0.05, vjust = 0.5, size = 3,
            family = "mono") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = break_df$order) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(y = "complete cases",
       x = "biomarker-addition step",
       title = "Sample-size attrition as biomarkers are added") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 1))

# 3 Figure 2: improved RF-importance plot


imp_plot <- ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# 4 Figure 3: coefficient plot with model stats in the strip
# tidy & label the final model  (albumin example shown)
coef_df <- tidy(model_selected_albumin, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%                # ← NEW: remove intercept
  mutate(
    term    = str_remove_all(term, "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"),
    term    = if_else(str_detect(term, ":"),         # italicise interactions
                      paste0("*", term, "*"), term),
    t_stat  = abs(statistic)                         # keep the t-value for plot 4
  )

model_stats <- glance(model_selected_albumin)

coef_plot <- ggplot(coef_df,
                    aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
                        x = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.15, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "β  (95 % CI)",
       title = glue("Linear model – Albumin (R² {round(model_stats$r.squared,3)}, n = {model_stats$nobs})")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# t-stat bar 

# Option 4: Combined plot with effect size context
t_plot <- coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    sig_level = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    sig_color = case_when(
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "Significant",
      p.value < 0.1  ~ "Marginal",
      TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
             x = statistic)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = sig_color)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig_level, 
                x = statistic + sign(statistic) * 0.3),
            size = 4, vjust = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Significant" = "#009E73",
                               "Marginal" = "#F0E442",
                               "Not significant" = "#999999"),
                    name = "Significance") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "t-statistic",
       title = "T-statistics with significance levels",
       subtitle = "*** p<0.001, ** p<0.01, * p<0.05, . p<0.1") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# 5 Table 1: breakpoint narrative

tbl_breaks <- break_df %>%
  group_by(order) %>%
  summarise(`complete cases` = first(n_cases),
            `added biomarkers` = paste(added_vars, collapse = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(step = row_number()) %>%
  select(step, everything()) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Sample-size breakpoints**")) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = `complete cases`, sep_mark = ",") %>%
  tab_style(cell_borders(sides = "top", weight = px(1)),
            locations = cells_body(rows = 1)) %>%
  opt_row_striping()

# 6 Table 2: model performance
tbl_perf <- tibble(
  outcome = "Albumin index",
  adj_R2  = round(model_stats$r.squared, 3),
  RMSE    = round(model_stats$sigma, 3),
  n       = model_stats$nobs
) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Final model performance**"))

# 7 Arrange and save / display


# side-by-side figures, adjust widths to taste
(break_plot | imp_plot | coef_plot | t_plot) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.2, 1, 1, 0.9))   # tweak widths as you like

# save
ggsave("albumin_analysis_plasmaOnly.png", width = 17, height = 4, dpi = 300)

# view tables in the Viewer or knit to Quarto
tbl_breaks
tbl_perf

```

Running albumin \~ csf_only …

```{r}

dat_sub|> dplyr::select(-fl_csf_igg_log2) -> dat_sub_without_igg

csf_markers <- dat_sub_without_igg %>%                 # start with your data
  select(                                             # pick columns …
    contains("csf"), 
    fl_albumin_index_log2                             # … and this one explicit name
  ) %>% 
  names()   

result <- e_data_complete_by_variable_subset(dat_sub_without_igg, var_list = csf_markers, var_resp = "fl_albumin_index_log2")
# See all variable names for each row
result$var_names

#         setdiff(current_vars, prev_vars)
#       }
#

#       clean_vars <- gsub("fl_csf__ALL__", "", new_vars)
#       clean_vars <- gsub("_log2", "", clean_vars)
#       
#       cat(sprintf("%d cases: %s\n", 
#                   current_n,
#                   if(i == 1) {
#                     sprintf("starting with %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))

#       
#       prev_vars <- current_vars
#       prev_n <- current_n
#     }
#   }
# }
# 
# # Apply the function to your groups
# summarize_breakpoints(log2_groups)
# 
# # Also show the complete list of final variables
# cat("\nFinal set of biomarkers:\n")
# final_vars <- gsub("fl_csf__ALL__", "", 
#                   gsub("_log2", "", 
#                        log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables[-1]))
# cat(paste(final_vars, collapse = "\n"))



# Apply RandomForest to check the important varibels for Albumin index with 7 cases and 61 Observation
# 6        108     5 "fl_albumin_index_log2 fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2                       fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2 fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2 fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2                                                                                                                                                                                          … 
# [1] "fl_albumin_index_log2"    "fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2"    "fl_msd_csf_mmp_2_log2"    "fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2"    "fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2"    "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2"    "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2"  
# [9] "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2"   74

 biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 # "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2",
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2",
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2",
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_2_log2" 
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
# "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)

# Function to run Random Forest analysis
run_rf_analysis <- function(data, outcome_var, predictors, n_trees = 500) {
  # Prepare data
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, predictors)])
  
  # Create formula
  rf_formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Run Random Forest
  set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
  rf_model <- randomForest(
    formula = rf_formula,
    data = model_data,
    ntree = n_trees,
    importance = TRUE
  )

    # Get variable importance
  var_imp <- importance(rf_model)
 var_imp_df <- data.frame(
  variable = rownames(var_imp),  # Keep original variable names
  variable_clean = gsub("fl_csf__ALL__", "", gsub("_log2", "", rownames(var_imp))),
  importance = var_imp[, "%IncMSE"],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
  var_imp_df <- var_imp_df[order(-var_imp_df$importance), ]
  
  # Calculate model performance
  predictions <- predict(rf_model) #If no new data is provided, it predicts for the training data.
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2))
  r2 <- 1 - sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2) / 
            sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - mean(model_data[[outcome_var]]))^2)
  
  return(list(
    model = rf_model,
    importance = var_imp_df,
    performance = list(
      rmse = rmse,
      r2 = r2,
      n_obs = nrow(model_data)
    )
  ))
}

# Run Random Forest analysis
rf_results <- run_rf_analysis(
  data = dat_sub_without_igg,
  outcome_var = "fl_albumin_index_log2",
  predictors = biomarkers
)

# Print results
cat("\nRandom Forest Model Performance:\n")
cat("R-squared:", round(rf_results$performance$r2, 3), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(rf_results$performance$rmse, 3), "\n")
cat("Number of observations:", rf_results$performance$n_obs, "\n")

# Print variable importance
cat("\nVariable Importance (top 10):\n")
print(head(rf_results$importance, 10))

# Plot variable importance
ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# Function to create models with top N variables
create_top_n_model <- function(data, outcome_var, importance_df, n_vars) {
  # Use the original variable names from the importance dataframe
  top_vars <- importance_df$variable[1:n_vars]
  
  # Create formula with interactions
  formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", 
                            paste(top_vars, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Fit model
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, top_vars)])
  model <- lm(formula, data = model_data)

    fml_upper <- as.formula(
  paste("outcome_var ~ (", paste(top_vars, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
 
     fml_lower <- as.formula("outcome_var ~ 1")
  
  # 6. Perform stepwise selection

     model_selected <- step(
  object  = model,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower, upper = fml_upper),
  direction = "both",
  trace = 0  # Suppress stepwise output
)

  return(model_selected)
}

# Create models with top 4 variables
top7_model <- create_top_n_model(
  data = dat_sub_without_igg,
  outcome_var = "fl_albumin_index_log2",
  importance_df = rf_results$importance,
  n_vars = 7
)

# Print results of top 4 model
cat("\nModel with top 7 variables from Random Forest:\n")
summary(top7_model)


# Now base on the result of Random-forest we create new albumin with 4 Var

 biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 # "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 # "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" 
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_sheet[, c("fl_albumin_index_log2", biomarkers)]))
print(n_complete) # Only one remained

# sapply(some_biomarkers_vd, function(x) sum(!is.na(dat_sheet[[x]])))



# 2. Build a formula: TripleDichotomy_VD ~ biomarker1 + biomarker2 + ...
albumin_form <- as.formula(
  paste("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "))
)

# Fit main-effects model
albumin_main <- lm(albumin_form, data = dat_sheet)

summary(albumin_main)


# car::Anova(mod_main, type = 3)
# # 
# e_plot_lm_diagnostics(mod_main, sw_plot_set = "simple")
# 
# # Perform Shapiro-Wilk test on the residuals
# shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(residuals(mod_main))


## -- 2. Define the 'upper' and 'lower' scopes for stepwise selection
#    Upper includes two-way interactions: (x1 + x2 + ...)^2
#    Lower is just an intercept-only model: ~ 1

fml_upper_albumin <- as.formula(
  paste("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ (", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
fml_lower_albumin <- as.formula("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ 1")

## -- 3. Run stepwise selection
model_selected_albumin <- step(
  object  = albumin_main,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower_albumin, upper = fml_upper_albumin),
  direction = "forward"
)



## -- 4. Inspect the final model
summary(model_selected_albumin)




e_plot_scatterplot( dat_plot = dat_sheet,
  var_x ="fl_csf_igg_log2",
  var_y = "fl_albumin_index_log2",
  smooth_all = c("lm")
  )



pal_okabe <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
               "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00",
               "#CC79A7", "#999999")


# turn your `log2_groups` list into a tidy frame
break_df <- imap_dfr(log2_groups, ~{
  tibble(
    order         = .y,
    n_cases       = .x$complete_cases,
    added_vars    = setdiff(.x$variables[-1],
                            if (.y == 1) character(0) else log2_groups[[.y-1]]$variables[-1])
  )
}) %>%
  unnest_longer(added_vars, keep_empty = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(added_vars = str_remove_all(added_vars,
                                     "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"))

# 2 Figure 1: attrition curve
break_plot <- ggplot(break_df,
                     aes(x = order, y = n_cases)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .8, colour = pal_okabe[2]) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour  = pal_okabe[1]) +
  geom_text(aes(label = added_vars),
            hjust = -0.05, vjust = 0.5, size = 3,
            family = "mono") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = break_df$order) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(y = "complete cases",
       x = "biomarker-addition step",
       title = "Sample-size attrition as biomarkers are added") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 1))

# 3 Figure 2: improved RF-importance plot


imp_plot <- ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# 4 Figure 3: coefficient plot with model stats in the strip
# tidy & label the final model  (albumin example shown)
coef_df <- tidy(model_selected_albumin, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%                # ← NEW: remove intercept
  mutate(
    term    = str_remove_all(term, "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"),
    term    = if_else(str_detect(term, ":"),         # italicise interactions
                      paste0("*", term, "*"), term),
    t_stat  = abs(statistic)                         # keep the t-value for plot 4
  )

model_stats <- glance(model_selected_albumin)

coef_plot <- ggplot(coef_df,
                    aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
                        x = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.15, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "β  (95 % CI)",
       title = glue("Linear model – Albumin (R² {round(model_stats$r.squared,3)}, n = {model_stats$nobs})")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# t-stat bar 

# Option 4: Combined plot with effect size context
t_plot <- coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    sig_level = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    sig_color = case_when(
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "Significant",
      p.value < 0.1  ~ "Marginal",
      TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
             x = statistic)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = sig_color)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig_level, 
                x = statistic + sign(statistic) * 0.3),
            size = 4, vjust = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Significant" = "#009E73",
                               "Marginal" = "#F0E442",
                               "Not significant" = "#999999"),
                    name = "Significance") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "t-statistic",
       title = "T-statistics with significance levels",
       subtitle = "*** p<0.001, ** p<0.01, * p<0.05, . p<0.1") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# 5 Table 1: breakpoint narrative

tbl_breaks <- break_df %>%
  group_by(order) %>%
  summarise(`complete cases` = first(n_cases),
            `added biomarkers` = paste(added_vars, collapse = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(step = row_number()) %>%
  select(step, everything()) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Sample-size breakpoints**")) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = `complete cases`, sep_mark = ",") %>%
  tab_style(cell_borders(sides = "top", weight = px(1)),
            locations = cells_body(rows = 1)) %>%
  opt_row_striping()

# 6 Table 2: model performance
tbl_perf <- tibble(
  outcome = "Albumin index",
  adj_R2  = round(model_stats$r.squared, 3),
  RMSE    = round(model_stats$sigma, 3),
  n       = model_stats$nobs
) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Final model performance**"))

# 7 Arrange and save / display



# 5 Table 1: breakpoint narrative

tbl_breaks <- break_df %>%
  group_by(order) %>%
  summarise(`complete cases` = first(n_cases),
            `added biomarkers` = paste(added_vars, collapse = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(step = row_number()) %>%
  select(step, everything()) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Sample-size breakpoints**")) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = `complete cases`, sep_mark = ",") %>%
  tab_style(cell_borders(sides = "top", weight = px(1)),
            locations = cells_body(rows = 1)) %>%
  opt_row_striping()

# 6 Table 2: model performance
tbl_perf <- tibble(
  outcome = "Albumin index",
  adj_R2  = round(model_stats$r.squared, 3),
  RMSE    = round(model_stats$sigma, 3),
  n       = model_stats$nobs
) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Final model performance**"))

# 7 Arrange and save / display

# side-by-side figures, adjust widths to taste
(break_plot | imp_plot | coef_plot | t_plot) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.2, 1, 1, 0.9))   # tweak widths as you like

# save
ggsave("albumin_analys_onlycsf_overview.png", width = 17, height = 4, dpi = 300)

# view tables in the Viewer or knit to Quarto
tbl_breaks
tbl_perf

```

Running albumin \~ plasma_csf …

```{r}
# 
# # Get only plasma biomarkers ending in _log2
# find_optimal_log2_groups <- function(data, min_cases = 30) {
#   # Get only plasma markers ending in _log2
#   plasma_csf_markers <- grep("^fl_(plasma|csf).*_log2$", names(data), value = TRUE)
#    
#   # Start with outcome variable
#   current_vars <- c("fl_albumin_index_log2")
#   results <- list()
#   
#   for(marker in plasma_csf_markers) {
#     temp_vars <- c(current_vars, marker)
#     complete_cases <- sum(complete.cases(data[, temp_vars]))
#     
#     if(complete_cases >= min_cases) {
#       current_vars <- temp_vars
#       results[[length(results) + 1]] <- list(
#         variables = current_vars,
#         complete_cases = complete_cases
#       )
#     }
#   }
#   return(results)
# }
# 
# # Apply the function
# log2_groups <- find_optimal_log2_groups(dat_sub)
# 
# # Print summary of available biomarkers and sample sizes
# for(i in seq_along(log2_groups)) {
#   cat("\nGroup", i, ":\n")
#   cat("Variables:", paste(log2_groups[[i]]$variables[-1], collapse = ", "), "\n")
#   cat("Complete cases:", log2_groups[[i]]$complete_cases, "\n")
# }
#

# final_log2_vars <- log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables
# 
# # Print final number of biomarkers and sample size
# cat("\nFinal model will include:", length(final_log2_vars)-1, "biomarkers\n")
# cat("With sample size:", log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$complete_cases, "\n")
# 
# # Function to print breakpoint summary
# summarize_breakpoints <- function(groups) {
#   prev_vars <- NULL
#   prev_n <- NULL
#   
#   cat("Sample Size Breakpoints:\n")
#   
#   for(i in seq_along(groups)) {
#     current_vars <- groups[[i]]$variables[-1]  # exclude outcome variable
#     current_n <- groups[[i]]$complete_cases
#     
#     if(is.null(prev_n) || current_n != prev_n) {
#       new_vars <- if(is.null(prev_vars)) {
#         current_vars
#       } else {
#         setdiff(current_vars, prev_vars)
#       }
#       
#       # Separate CSF and plasma variables
#       csf_vars <- grep("csf", new_vars, value = TRUE)
#       plasma_vars <- grep("plasma", new_vars, value = TRUE)
#       
#       # Clean up variable names for display but keep CSF/Plasma indicator
#       clean_vars <- gsub("^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__", "\\1: ", new_vars)
#       clean_vars <- gsub("^fl_csf_", "csf: ", clean_vars)
#       clean_vars <- gsub("^fl_plasma_", "plasma: ", clean_vars)
#       clean_vars <- gsub("_log2", "", clean_vars)
#       
#       cat(sprintf("%d cases: %s\n", 
#                   current_n,
#                   if(i == 1) {
#                     sprintf("starting with %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
#                   } else {
#                     sprintf("adding %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
#                   }))
#       
#       prev_vars <- current_vars
#       prev_n <- current_n
#     }
#   }
# }
# 
# # Apply the function to your groups
# summarize_breakpoints(log2_groups)
# 
# # Also show the complete list of final variables
# cat("\nFinal set of biomarkers:\n")
# 
# # Separate CSF and plasma for final display
# final_vars <- log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables[-1]
# csf_vars <- grep("csf", final_vars, value = TRUE)
# plasma_vars <- grep("plasma", final_vars, value = TRUE)
# 
# cat("\nCSF biomarkers (", length(csf_vars), "):\n", sep = "")
# csf_clean <- gsub("^fl_(csf)__ALL__", "", csf_vars)
# csf_clean <- gsub("^fl_csf_", "", csf_clean)
# csf_clean <- gsub("_log2", "", csf_clean)
# cat(paste(csf_clean, collapse = "\n"))
# 
# cat("\n\nPlasma biomarkers (", length(plasma_vars), "):\n", sep = "")
# plasma_clean <- gsub("^fl_(plasma)__ALL__", "", plasma_vars)
# plasma_clean <- gsub("^fl_plasma_", "", plasma_clean)
# plasma_clean <- gsub("_log2", "", plasma_clean)
# cat(paste(plasma_clean, collapse = "\n"))



dat_sub|> dplyr::select(-fl_csf_igg_log2) -> dat_sub_without_igg

csf_plasma_markers <- dat_sub_without_igg |>
  select(
    contains("csf"),
    contains("plasma"),
    fl_albumin_index_log2
  ) %>%
  names()
result <- e_data_complete_by_variable_subset(dat_sub_without_igg, var_list = csf_plasma_markers, var_resp = "fl_albumin_index_log2")
# See all variable names for each row
result$var_names[8]


# Apply RandomForest to check the important varibels for Albumin index with 40 cases both plasma and csf
# [1] "fl_albumin_index_log2"      "fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2"      "fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2"      "fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2"      "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2"   "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2"


 biomarkers<- c(
  "fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2" ,
  "fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2" ,
  "fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2"  ,
  "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2"
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 # "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
#  "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
#  "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
#  "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" 
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)



n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_sub_without_igg[, c("fl_albumin_index_log2", biomarkers)]))
print(n_complete) # Only one remained

library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)

# Function to run Random Forest analysis
run_rf_analysis <- function(data, outcome_var, predictors, n_trees = 500) {
  # Prepare data
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, predictors)])
  
  # Create formula
  rf_formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Run Random Forest
  set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
  rf_model <- randomForest(
    formula = rf_formula,
    data = model_data,
    ntree = n_trees,
    importance = TRUE
  )

    # Get variable importance
  var_imp <- importance(rf_model)
  var_imp_df <- data.frame(
  variable = rownames(var_imp),  # CHANGE: Keep original names
  variable_clean = gsub("^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__", "", gsub("_log2", "", rownames(var_imp))), # For display
  importance = var_imp[, "%IncMSE"],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
  var_imp_df <- var_imp_df[order(-var_imp_df$importance), ]
  
  # Calculate model performance
  predictions <- predict(rf_model) #If no new data is provided, it predicts for the training data.
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2))
  r2 <- 1 - sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2) / 
            sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - mean(model_data[[outcome_var]]))^2)
  
  return(list(
    model = rf_model,
    importance = var_imp_df,
    performance = list(
      rmse = rmse,
      r2 = r2,
      n_obs = nrow(model_data)
    )
  ))
}

# Run Random Forest analysis
rf_results <- run_rf_analysis(
  data = dat_sub_without_igg,
  outcome_var = "fl_albumin_index_log2",
  predictors = biomarkers
)

# Print results
cat("\nRandom Forest Model Performance:\n")
cat("R-squared:", round(rf_results$performance$r2, 3), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(rf_results$performance$rmse, 3), "\n")
cat("Number of observations:", rf_results$performance$n_obs, "\n")

# Print variable importance
cat("\nVariable Importance (top 10):\n")
print(head(rf_results$importance, 10))

# Plot variable importance
ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# Function to create models with top N variables
create_top_n_model <- function(data, outcome_var, importance_df, n_vars) {
  top_vars <- top_vars <- importance_df$variable[1:n_vars]

  
  # Create formula with interactions
  formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", 
                            paste(top_vars, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Fit model
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, top_vars)])
  model <- lm(formula, data = model_data)

  fml_upper <- as.formula(
  paste(outcome_var, "~ (", paste(top_vars, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
  )
 
     fml_lower <- as.formula("outcome_var ~ 1")
  
  # 6. Perform stepwise selection

     model_selected <- step(
  object  = model,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower, upper = fml_upper),
  direction = "both",
  trace = 0  # Suppress stepwise output
)

  return(model_selected)
}

# Create models with top 7 variables
top3_model <- create_top_n_model(
  data = dat_sub_without_igg,
  outcome_var = "fl_albumin_index_log2",
  importance_df = rf_results$importance,
  n_vars = 3
)

# Print results of top 4 model
cat("\nModel with top 3 variables from Random Forest:\n")
summary(top3_model)


# Now base on the result of Random-forest we create new albumin



library(dplyr)

# 1. Extract columns that start with either "fl_plasma_" or "fl_csf_"
# biomarker_cols <- dat_all_combine %>%
#   dplyr::select(matches("^(fl_plasma_|fl_csf_|fl_simoa_|fl_msd_|fl_abeta_).*log2$")) %>%
#   names()


# 
# biomarker_cols <- dat_all_combine_AD_VD %>%
#   dplyr::select(matches("^(fl_plasma_|fl_csf_|fl_abeta_38).*log2$")) %>%
#   names()
# 
# biomarker_cols_final <- biomarker_cols[ !grepl("original", biomarker_cols) ]
# 
# # Number of complete rows for the variables of interest:
# # n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_all_combine[, c("TripleDichotomy_VD_log2", biomarker_cols)]))
# # print(n_complete) # Only one remained
# 
# # how many rows remain for each biomarker individually:
# sapply(biomarker_cols, function(x) sum(!is.na(dat_all_combine_AD_VD[[x]])))
# 
# e_data_complete_by_variable_subset(dat_all_combine_AD_VD, var_list = c(some_biomarkers_vd, "fl_albumin_index_log2"), var_resp = "fl_albumin_index_log2")
 
# if you choose this you will get lots of interaction but Rsquare still low around 40%
# 5 Important varibels base on the result of RF IMportance
biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 # "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",         
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",        
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" 
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2"
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_sheet[, c("fl_albumin_index_log2", biomarkers)]))
print(n_complete) # Only one remained

# sapply(some_biomarkers_vd, function(x) sum(!is.na(dat_sheet[[x]])))



# 2. Build a formula: TripleDichotomy_VD ~ biomarker1 + biomarker2 + ...
albumin_form <- as.formula(
  paste("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "))
)

# Fit main-effects model
albumin_main <- lm(albumin_form, data = dat_sheet)

summary(albumin_main)


# car::Anova(mod_main, type = 3)
# # 
# e_plot_lm_diagnostics(mod_main, sw_plot_set = "simple")
# 
# # Perform Shapiro-Wilk test on the residuals
# shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(residuals(mod_main))


## -- 2. Define the 'upper' and 'lower' scopes for stepwise selection
#    Upper includes two-way interactions: (x1 + x2 + ...)^2
#    Lower is just an intercept-only model: ~ 1

fml_upper_albumin <- as.formula(
  paste("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ (", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
fml_lower_albumin <- as.formula("fl_albumin_index_log2 ~ 1")

## -- 3. Run stepwise selection
model_selected_albumin <- step(
  object  = albumin_main,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower_albumin, upper = fml_upper_albumin),
  direction = "forward"
)



## -- 4. Inspect the final model
summary(model_selected_albumin)



pal_okabe <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
               "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00",
               "#CC79A7", "#999999")

# 1 Break-point data → nice tibble


# turn your `log2_groups` list into a tidy frame
break_df <- imap_dfr(log2_groups, ~{
  tibble(
    order         = .y,
    n_cases       = .x$complete_cases,
    added_vars    = setdiff(.x$variables[-1],
                            if (.y == 1) character(0) else log2_groups[[.y-1]]$variables[-1])
  )
}) %>%
  unnest_longer(added_vars, keep_empty = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(added_vars = str_remove_all(added_vars,
                                     "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"))

# 2 Figure 1: attrition curve
break_plot <- ggplot(break_df,
                     aes(x = order, y = n_cases)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .8, colour = pal_okabe[2]) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour  = pal_okabe[1]) +
  geom_text(aes(label = added_vars),
            hjust = -0.05, vjust = 0.5, size = 3,
            family = "mono") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = break_df$order) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(y = "complete cases",
       x = "biomarker-addition step",
       title = "Sample-size attrition as biomarkers are added") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 1))

# 3 Figure 2: improved RF-importance plot


imp_plot <- ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# 4 Figure 3: coefficient plot with model stats in the strip
# tidy & label the final model  (albumin example shown)
coef_df <- tidy(model_selected_albumin, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%                # ← NEW: remove intercept
  mutate(
    term    = str_remove_all(term, "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"),
    term    = if_else(str_detect(term, ":"),         # italicise interactions
                      paste0("*", term, "*"), term),
    t_stat  = abs(statistic)                         # keep the t-value for plot 4
  )

model_stats <- glance(model_selected_albumin)

coef_plot <- ggplot(coef_df,
                    aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
                        x = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.15, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "β  (95 % CI)",
       title = glue("Linear model – Albumin (R² {round(model_stats$r.squared,3)}, n = {model_stats$nobs})")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# t-stat bar 

# Option 4: Combined plot with effect size context
t_plot <- coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    sig_level = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    sig_color = case_when(
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "Significant",
      p.value < 0.1  ~ "Marginal",
      TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
             x = statistic)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = sig_color)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig_level, 
                x = statistic + sign(statistic) * 0.3),
            size = 4, vjust = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Significant" = "#009E73",
                               "Marginal" = "#F0E442",
                               "Not significant" = "#999999"),
                    name = "Significance") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "t-statistic",
       title = "T-statistics with significance levels",
       subtitle = "*** p<0.001, ** p<0.01, * p<0.05, . p<0.1") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# 5 Table 1: breakpoint narrative

tbl_breaks <- break_df %>%
  group_by(order) %>%
  summarise(`complete cases` = first(n_cases),
            `added biomarkers` = paste(added_vars, collapse = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(step = row_number()) %>%
  select(step, everything()) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Sample-size breakpoints**")) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = `complete cases`, sep_mark = ",") %>%
  tab_style(cell_borders(sides = "top", weight = px(1)),
            locations = cells_body(rows = 1)) %>%
  opt_row_striping()

# 6 Table 2: model performance
tbl_perf <- tibble(
  outcome = "Albumin index",
  adj_R2  = round(model_stats$r.squared, 3),
  RMSE    = round(model_stats$sigma, 3),
  n       = model_stats$nobs
) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Final model performance**"))

# 7 Arrange and save / display


# side-by-side figures, adjust widths to taste
(break_plot | imp_plot | coef_plot | t_plot) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.2, 1, 1, 0.9))   # tweak widths as you like

# save
ggsave("albumin_analys_csf&plasma_overview.png", width = 17, height = 4, dpi = 300)

# view tables in the Viewer or knit to Quarto
tbl_breaks
tbl_perf




```

Running mri3t \~ plasma_only …

```{r}

# Get only plasma biomarkers ending in _log2
find_optimal_log2_groups <- function(data, min_cases = 30) {
  # Get only plasma markers ending in _log2
  plasma_markers <- grep("^fl_plasma.*_log2$", names(data), value = TRUE)
  
  # Start with outcome variable
  current_vars <- c("mri_perm_3t_tot")
  results <- list()
  
  for(marker in plasma_markers) {
    temp_vars <- c(current_vars, marker)
    complete_cases <- sum(complete.cases(data[, temp_vars]))
    
    if(complete_cases >= min_cases) {
      current_vars <- temp_vars
      results[[length(results) + 1]] <- list(
        variables = current_vars,
        complete_cases = complete_cases
      )
    }
  }
  return(results)
}

# Apply the function
log2_groups <- find_optimal_log2_groups(dat_sub)

# Print summary of available biomarkers and sample sizes
for(i in seq_along(log2_groups)) {
  cat("\nGroup", i, ":\n")
  cat("Variables:", paste(log2_groups[[i]]$variables[-1], collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("Complete cases:", log2_groups[[i]]$complete_cases, "\n")
}

# Get the final set of all log2 biomarkers
final_log2_vars <- log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables

# Print final number of biomarkers and sample size
cat("\nFinal model will include:", length(final_log2_vars)-1, "biomarkers\n")
cat("With sample size:", log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$complete_cases, "\n")

# Function to print breakpoint summary
summarize_breakpoints <- function(groups) {
  prev_vars <- NULL
  prev_n <- NULL
  
  cat("Sample Size Breakpoints:\n")
  
  for(i in seq_along(groups)) {
    current_vars <- groups[[i]]$variables[-1]  # exclude outcome variable
    current_n <- groups[[i]]$complete_cases
    
    if(is.null(prev_n) || current_n != prev_n) {
      new_vars <- if(is.null(prev_vars)) {
        current_vars
      } else {
        setdiff(current_vars, prev_vars)
      }
      
      # Clean up variable names for display
      clean_vars <- gsub("fl_plasma__ALL__", "", new_vars)
      clean_vars <- gsub("_log2", "", clean_vars)
      
      cat(sprintf("%d cases: %s\n", 
                  current_n,
                  if(i == 1) {
                    sprintf("starting with %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
                  } else {
                    sprintf("adding %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
                  }))
      
      prev_vars <- current_vars
      prev_n <- current_n
    }
  }
}

# Apply the function to your groups
summarize_breakpoints(log2_groups)

# Also show the complete list of final variables
cat("\nFinal set of biomarkers:\n")
final_vars <- gsub("fl_plasma__ALL__", "", 
                  gsub("_log2", "", 
                       log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables[-1]))
cat(paste(final_vars, collapse = "\n"))



# Apply RandomForest to check the important Var base on 10 var and 54 Observation



 biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 # "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",         
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",        
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 # "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,     
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2"
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)

# Function to run Random Forest analysis
run_rf_analysis <- function(data, outcome_var, predictors, n_trees = 500) {
  # Prepare data
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, predictors)])
  
  # Create formula
  rf_formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Run Random Forest
  set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
  rf_model <- randomForest(
    formula = rf_formula,
    data = model_data,
    ntree = n_trees,
    importance = TRUE
  )

    # Get variable importance
  var_imp <- importance(rf_model)
  var_imp_df <- data.frame(
    variable = gsub("fl_plasma__ALL__", "", gsub("_log2", "", rownames(var_imp))),
    importance = var_imp[, "%IncMSE"],  # Using %IncMSE as importance measure
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  var_imp_df <- var_imp_df[order(-var_imp_df$importance), ]
  
  # Calculate model performance
  predictions <- predict(rf_model) #If no new data is provided, it predicts for the training data.
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2))
  r2 <- 1 - sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2) / 
            sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - mean(model_data[[outcome_var]]))^2)
  
  return(list(
    model = rf_model,
    importance = var_imp_df,
    performance = list(
      rmse = rmse,
      r2 = r2,
      n_obs = nrow(model_data)
    )
  ))
}

# Run Random Forest analysis
rf_results <- run_rf_analysis(
  data = dat_sub,
  outcome_var = "mri_perm_3t_tot",
  predictors = biomarkers
)

# Print results
cat("\nRandom Forest Model Performance:\n")
cat("R-squared:", round(rf_results$performance$r2, 3), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(rf_results$performance$rmse, 3), "\n")
cat("Number of observations:", rf_results$performance$n_obs, "\n")

# Print variable importance
cat("\nVariable Importance (top 10):\n")
print(head(rf_results$importance, 10))

# Plot variable importance
ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# Function to create models with top N variables
create_top_n_model <- function(data, outcome_var, importance_df, n_vars) {
  top_vars <- paste0("fl_plasma__ALL__", 
                    importance_df$variable[1:n_vars], 
                    "_log2")
  
  # Create formula with interactions
  formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", 
                            paste(top_vars, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Fit model
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, top_vars)])
  model <- lm(formula, data = model_data)

    fml_upper <- as.formula(
  paste("outcome_var ~ (", paste(top_vars, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
 
     fml_lower <- as.formula("outcome_var ~ 1")
  
  # 6. Perform stepwise selection

     model_selected <- step(
  object  = model,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower, upper = fml_upper),
  direction = "both",
  trace = 0  # Suppress stepwise output
)

  return(model_selected)
}

# Create models with top 4 variables
top6_model <- create_top_n_model(
  data = dat_sub,
  outcome_var = "mri_perm_3t_tot",
  importance_df = rf_results$importance,
  n_vars = 6
)

# Print results of top 4 model
cat("\nModel with top 5 variables from Random Forest:\n")
summary(top6_model)


# Now base on the result of Random-forest we create new albumin



# if you choose this you will get lots of interaction but Rsquare still low around 40%
# 5 Important varibels base on the result of RF IMportance
biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 # "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",         
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",        
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 # "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,     
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" ,
 "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
"fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
 "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2"
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_sheet[, c("mri_perm_3t_tot", biomarkers)]))
print(n_complete) # Only one remained

# sapply(some_biomarkers_vd, function(x) sum(!is.na(dat_sheet[[x]])))



# 2. Build a formula: TripleDichotomy_VD ~ biomarker1 + biomarker2 + ...
mri_form <- as.formula(
  paste("mri_perm_3t_tot ~", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "))
)

# Fit main-effects model
mri_main <- lm(mri_form, data = dat_sheet)

summary(mri_main)


# car::Anova(mod_main, type = 3)
# # 
# e_plot_lm_diagnostics(mod_main, sw_plot_set = "simple")
# 
# # Perform Shapiro-Wilk test on the residuals
# shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(residuals(mod_main))


## -- 2. Define the 'upper' and 'lower' scopes for stepwise selection
#    Upper includes two-way interactions: (x1 + x2 + ...)^2
#    Lower is just an intercept-only model: ~ 1

fml_upper_mri <- as.formula(
  paste("mri_perm_3t_tot ~ (", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
fml_lower_mri <- as.formula("mri_perm_3t_tot ~ 1")

## -- 3. Run stepwise selection
model_selected_albumin <- step(
  object  = mri_main,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower_mri, upper = fml_upper_mri),
  direction = "forward"
)



## -- 4. Inspect the final model
summary(model_selected_albumin)




pal_okabe <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
               "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00",
               "#CC79A7", "#999999")

# 1 Break-point data → nice tibble


# turn your `log2_groups` list into a tidy frame
break_df <- imap_dfr(log2_groups, ~{
  tibble(
    order         = .y,
    n_cases       = .x$complete_cases,
    added_vars    = setdiff(.x$variables[-1],
                            if (.y == 1) character(0) else log2_groups[[.y-1]]$variables[-1])
  )
}) %>%
  unnest_longer(added_vars, keep_empty = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(added_vars = str_remove_all(added_vars,
                                     "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"))

# 2 Figure 1: attrition curve
break_plot <- ggplot(break_df,
                     aes(x = order, y = n_cases)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .8, colour = pal_okabe[2]) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour  = pal_okabe[1]) +
  geom_text(aes(label = added_vars),
            hjust = -0.05, vjust = 0.5, size = 3,
            family = "mono") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = break_df$order) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(y = "complete cases",
       x = "biomarker-addition step",
       title = "Sample-size attrition as biomarkers are added") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 1))

# 3 Figure 2: improved RF-importance plot


imp_plot <- ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# 4 Figure 3: coefficient plot with model stats in the strip
# tidy & label the final model  (albumin example shown)
coef_df <- tidy(model_selected_albumin, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%                # ← NEW: remove intercept
  mutate(
    term    = str_remove_all(term, "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"),
    term    = if_else(str_detect(term, ":"),         # italicise interactions
                      paste0("*", term, "*"), term),
    t_stat  = abs(statistic)                         # keep the t-value for plot 4
  )

model_stats <- glance(model_selected_albumin)

coef_plot <- ggplot(coef_df,
                    aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
                        x = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.15, colour = pal_okabe[5]) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "β  (95 % CI)",
       title = glue("Linear model – Albumin (R² {round(model_stats$r.squared,3)}, n = {model_stats$nobs})")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# t-stat bar 

# Option 4: Combined plot with effect size context
t_plot <- coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    sig_level = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    sig_color = case_when(
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "Significant",
      p.value < 0.1  ~ "Marginal",
      TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_reorder(term, estimate),
             x = statistic)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = sig_color)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig_level, 
                x = statistic + sign(statistic) * 0.3),
            size = 4, vjust = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Significant" = "#009E73",
                               "Marginal" = "#F0E442",
                               "Not significant" = "#999999"),
                    name = "Significance") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "t-statistic",
       title = "T-statistics with significance levels",
       subtitle = "*** p<0.001, ** p<0.01, * p<0.05, . p<0.1") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# 5 Table 1: breakpoint narrative

tbl_breaks <- break_df %>%
  group_by(order) %>%
  summarise(`complete cases` = first(n_cases),
            `added biomarkers` = paste(added_vars, collapse = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(step = row_number()) %>%
  select(step, everything()) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Sample-size breakpoints**")) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = `complete cases`, sep_mark = ",") %>%
  tab_style(cell_borders(sides = "top", weight = px(1)),
            locations = cells_body(rows = 1)) %>%
  opt_row_striping()

# 6 Table 2: model performance
tbl_perf <- tibble(
  outcome = "Albumin index",
  adj_R2  = round(model_stats$r.squared, 3),
  RMSE    = round(model_stats$sigma, 3),
  n       = model_stats$nobs
) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = md("**Final model performance**"))

# 7 Arrange and save / display


# side-by-side figures, adjust widths to taste
(break_plot | imp_plot | coef_plot | t_plot) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.2, 1, 1, 0.9))   # tweak widths as you like

# save
ggsave("mri_analys_onlyplasma_overview.png", width = 17, height = 4, dpi = 300)

# view tables in the Viewer or knit to Quarto
tbl_breaks
tbl_perf
```

Running mri3t \~ csf_only …

```{r}
# Get only plasma biomarkers ending in _log2
# find_optimal_log2_groups <- function(data, min_cases = 30) {
#   # Get only plasma markers ending in _log2
#   csf_markers <- grep("^fl_csf.*_log2$", names(data), value = TRUE)
#   
#   # Start with outcome variable
#   current_vars <- c("mri_perm_3t_tot")
#   results <- list()
#   
#   for(marker in csf_markers) {
#     temp_vars <- c(current_vars, marker)
#     complete_cases <- sum(complete.cases(data[, temp_vars]))
#     
#     if(complete_cases >= min_cases) {
#       current_vars <- temp_vars
#       results[[length(results) + 1]] <- list(
#         variables = current_vars,
#         complete_cases = complete_cases
#       )
#     }
#   }
#   return(results)
# }
# 
# # Apply the function
# log2_groups <- find_optimal_log2_groups(dat_sub)
# 
# # Print summary of available biomarkers and sample sizes
# for(i in seq_along(log2_groups)) {
#   cat("\nGroup", i, ":\n")
#   cat("Variables:", paste(log2_groups[[i]]$variables[-1], collapse = ", "), "\n")
#   cat("Complete cases:", log2_groups[[i]]$complete_cases, "\n")
# }
# 
# # Get the final set of all log2 biomarkers
# final_log2_vars <- log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables
# 
# # Print final number of biomarkers and sample size
# cat("\nFinal model will include:", length(final_log2_vars)-1, "biomarkers\n")
# cat("With sample size:", log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$complete_cases, "\n")
# 
# # Function to print breakpoint summary
# summarize_breakpoints <- function(groups) {
#   prev_vars <- NULL
#   prev_n <- NULL
#   
#   cat("Sample Size Breakpoints:\n")
#   
#   for(i in seq_along(groups)) {
#     current_vars <- groups[[i]]$variables[-1]  # exclude outcome variable
#     current_n <- groups[[i]]$complete_cases
#     
#     if(is.null(prev_n) || current_n != prev_n) {
#       new_vars <- if(is.null(prev_vars)) {
#         current_vars
#       } else {
#         setdiff(current_vars, prev_vars)
#       }
#       
#       # Clean up variable names for display
#       clean_vars <- gsub("fl_csf__ALL__", "", new_vars)
#       clean_vars <- gsub("_log2", "", clean_vars)
#       
#       cat(sprintf("%d cases: %s\n", 
#                   current_n,
#                   if(i == 1) {
#                     sprintf("starting with %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
#                   } else {
#                     sprintf("adding %s", paste(clean_vars, collapse = " and "))
#                   }))
#       
#       prev_vars <- current_vars
#       prev_n <- current_n
#     }
#   }
# }
# 
# # Apply the function to your groups
# summarize_breakpoints(log2_groups)
# 
# # Also show the complete list of final variables
# cat("\nFinal set of biomarkers:\n")
# final_vars <- gsub("fl_csf__ALL__", "", 
#                   gsub("_log2", "", 
#                        log2_groups[[length(log2_groups)]]$variables[-1]))
# cat(paste(final_vars, collapse = "\n"))
# 



dat_sub|> dplyr::select(-fl_csf_igg_log2) -> dat_sub_without_igg

csf_mri_markers <- dat_sub_without_igg |>
  select(
    contains("csf"),
    mri_perm_3t_tot
  ) %>%
  names()
result <- e_data_complete_by_variable_subset(dat_sub_without_igg, var_list = csf_mri_markers, var_resp = "mri_perm_3t_tot")
# See all variable names for each row
result$var_names[5]


# Apply RandomForest to check the important varibels for mri, 7 cases and 38 Observation

# [[1]]
# [1] "mri_perm_3t_tot"          "fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2"    "fl_msd_csf_mmp_2_log2"    "fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2"    "fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2"    "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2"  
# 51

 biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
#  "fl_csf_igg_log2",
#  # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
#  # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
#  # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
#  "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
#  "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
#  "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
#  # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
#  # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
#  # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
#  "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
# #  "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
#  "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" ,
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_1_log2",
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_2_log2",
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_3_log2",
 "fl_msd_csf_mmp_9_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2"
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)

# Function to run Random Forest analysis
run_rf_analysis <- function(data, outcome_var, predictors, n_trees = 500) {
  # Prepare data
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, predictors)])
  
  # Create formula
  rf_formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Run Random Forest
  set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
  rf_model <- randomForest(
    formula = rf_formula,
    data = model_data,
    ntree = n_trees,
    importance = TRUE
  )

    # Get variable importance
  var_imp <- importance(rf_model)
 var_imp_df <- data.frame(
  variable = rownames(var_imp),  # Keep original variable names
  variable_clean = gsub("fl_csf__ALL__", "", gsub("_log2", "", rownames(var_imp))),
  importance = var_imp[, "%IncMSE"],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
  var_imp_df <- var_imp_df[order(-var_imp_df$importance), ]
  
  # Calculate model performance
  predictions <- predict(rf_model) #If no new data is provided, it predicts for the training data.
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2))
  r2 <- 1 - sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - predictions)^2) / 
            sum((model_data[[outcome_var]] - mean(model_data[[outcome_var]]))^2)
  
  return(list(
    model = rf_model,
    importance = var_imp_df,
    performance = list(
      rmse = rmse,
      r2 = r2,
      n_obs = nrow(model_data)
    )
  ))
}

# Run Random Forest analysis
rf_results <- run_rf_analysis(
  data = dat_sub_without_igg,
  outcome_var = "mri_perm_3t_tot",
  predictors = biomarkers
)

# Print results
cat("\nRandom Forest Model Performance:\n")
cat("R-squared:", round(rf_results$performance$r2, 3), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(rf_results$performance$rmse, 3), "\n")
cat("Number of observations:", rf_results$performance$n_obs, "\n")

# Print variable importance
cat("\nVariable Importance (top 10):\n")
print(head(rf_results$importance, 10))

# Plot variable importance
ggplot(rf_results$importance, aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance in Random Forest Model",
    x = "Variables",
    y = "Importance (%IncMSE)"
  )

# Function to create models with top N variables
create_top_n_model <- function(data, outcome_var, importance_df, n_vars) {
  # Use the original variable names from the importance dataframe
  top_vars <- importance_df$variable[1:n_vars]
  
  # Create formula with interactions
  formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", 
                            paste(top_vars, collapse = " + ")))
  
  # Fit model
  model_data <- na.omit(data[, c(outcome_var, top_vars)])
  model <- lm(formula, data = model_data)

    fml_upper <- as.formula(
  paste("outcome_var ~ (", paste(top_vars, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
 
     fml_lower <- as.formula("outcome_var ~ 1")
  
  # 6. Perform stepwise selection

     model_selected <- step(
  object  = model,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower, upper = fml_upper),
  direction = "both",
  trace = 0  # Suppress stepwise output
)

  return(model_selected)
}

# Create models with top 4 variables
top2_model <- create_top_n_model(
  data = dat_sub_without_igg,
  outcome_var = "mri_perm_3t_tot",
  importance_df = rf_results$importance,
  n_vars = 2
)

# Print results of top 4 model
cat("\nModel with top 2 variables from Random Forest:\n")
summary(top2_model)


# Now base on the result of Random-forest we create new albumin



library(dplyr)

# 1. Extract columns that start with either "fl_plasma_" or "fl_csf_"
# biomarker_cols <- dat_all_combine %>%
#   dplyr::select(matches("^(fl_plasma_|fl_csf_|fl_simoa_|fl_msd_|fl_abeta_).*log2$")) %>%
#   names()




 
# if you choose this you will get lots of interaction but Rsquare still low around 40%
# 5 Important varibels base on the result of RF IMportance

 biomarkers<- c(
 # "fl_csf_glucose_log2",
 # "fl_csf_total_protein_log2",
 # "fl_csf_rbc_log2",
 # "fl_csf_tnc_log2",
 "fl_csf_igg_log2",
 # "fl_csf_albumin_log2",           
 # "fl_csf_igg_synthesis_log2",     
 # "fl_abeta_38_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__gfap_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__gfap_log2",    
 "fl_csf__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__nfl_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__ptau_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_181_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta42_40_ratio_log2",
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__ptau_217_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_40_log2",   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_40_log2",
 # # "fl_csf__ALL__abeta_42_log2" ,   
 # "fl_plasma__ALL__abeta_42_log2",
 # "fl_csf__ALL__plgf_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__plgf_log2" ,
 "fl_csf__ALL__il_1b_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__il_1b_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_1_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_3_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_9_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__mmp_10_log2",
 "fl_csf__ALL__mmp_10_log2" 
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__bfgf_log2",
# "fl_plasma__ALL__flt_1_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__tie2_log2" ,
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_a_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_c_log2",
#  "fl_plasma__ALL__vegf_d_log2"

)




n_complete <- sum(complete.cases(dat_sheet[, c("mri_perm_3t_tot", biomarkers)]))
print(n_complete) # Only one remained

# sapply(some_biomarkers_vd, function(x) sum(!is.na(dat_sheet[[x]])))



# 2. Build a formula: TripleDichotomy_VD ~ biomarker1 + biomarker2 + ...
mri_form <- as.formula(
  paste("mri_perm_3t_tot ~", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "))
)

# Fit main-effects model
mri_main <- lm(mri_form, data = dat_sheet)

summary(mri_main)


# car::Anova(mod_main, type = 3)
# # 
# e_plot_lm_diagnostics(mod_main, sw_plot_set = "simple")
# 
# # Perform Shapiro-Wilk test on the residuals
# shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(residuals(mod_main))


## -- 2. Define the 'upper' and 'lower' scopes for stepwise selection
#    Upper includes two-way interactions: (x1 + x2 + ...)^2
#    Lower is just an intercept-only model: ~ 1

fml_upper_mri <- as.formula(
  paste("mri_perm_3t_tot ~ (", paste(biomarkers, collapse = " + "), ")^2")
)
fml_lower_mri <- as.formula("mri_perm_3t_tot ~ 1")

## -- 3. Run stepwise selection
model_selected_albumin <- step(
  object  = mri_main,
  scope   = list(lower = fml_lower_mri, upper = fml_upper_mri),
  direction = "forward"
)



## -- 4. Inspect the final model
summary(model_selected_albumin)


pal_okabe <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
               "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00",
               "#CC79A7", "#999999")

# 1 Break-point data → nice tibble


# turn your `log2_groups` list into a tidy frame
break_df <- imap_dfr(log2_groups, ~{
  tibble(
    order         = .y,
    n_cases       = .x$complete_cases,
    added_vars    = setdiff(.x$variables[-1],
                            if (.y == 1) character(0) else log2_groups[[.y-1]]$variables[-1])
  )
}) %>%
  unnest_longer(added_vars, keep_empty = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(added_vars = str_remove_all(added_vars,
                                     "^fl_(plasma|csf)__ALL__|_log2$"))

# 2 Figure 1: attrition curve
break_plot <- ggplot(break_df,
                     aes(x = order, y = n_cases)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .8, colour = pal_okabe[2]) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour  = pal_okabe[1]) +
  geom_text(aes(label = added_vars),
            hjust = -0.05, vjust = 0.5, size = 3,
            family = "mono") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = break_df$order) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(y = "complete cases",
       x = "biomarker-addition step",
       title = "Sample-size attrition as biomarkers are added") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
```
